description_long: This object consists of two large, vertically-oriented cylindrical storage tanks placed side by side. Each tank features a series of horizontal ribbed bands around its circumference, likely for structural reinforcement. The tanks have flat bases and slightly domed tops with a central circular hatch or lid, which may be used for filling or maintenance access. One tank is predominantly white with visible dirt and weathering near the base, while the other is black and appears cleaner. The tanks are made of molded plastic, a common material for water or chemical storage in urban environments. There are no visible ladders, pipes, or external attachments, and the tanks are free-standing with no supporting structures.
description: Two large, ribbed cylindrical storage tanks—one white and one black—stand side by side, each with a domed top and central hatch. The tanks are made of molded plastic and show signs of outdoor use.
description_view_0: Front view showing both tanks side by side, with the white tank on the left and the black tank on the right; ribbing and top hatches are clearly visible.
description_view_1: Oblique view from behind, with the black tank in front and the white tank partially obscured behind; emphasizes the cylindrical shape and ribbing.
description_view_2: Rear view with the black tank on the left and the white tank on the right; both tanks' backs and ribbing are visible.
description_view_3: Oblique view from the opposite side, with the white tank in front and the black tank partially behind; the white tank's weathered base is prominent.
